items.title: RV Inspection
items.h1: RV Water Leak Inspection in Gatineau
items.serviceType: RV Water Leak Inspection and Moisture Testing
items.seoTitle: RV Water Leak Inspection in Gatineau | Seals, Roof and Moisture
items.seoDescription: RV water leak inspection in Gatineau with moisture meter testing, focused on seals, roof condition, moisture and water intrusion for Gatineau, Outaouais, Ottawa and the Laurentides.
items.opening: Clinique du VR provides visual RV water-leak inspection in Gatineau with moisture meter testing, with a strong focus on seals, roof condition, and signs of water intrusion. Our goal is to quickly identify risk zones, moisture issues, and sealing problems before they lead to more damage.
items.lead: Our inspections are mainly designed to detect issues with seals, the roof, and water intrusion. We do not perform mechanical inspections. We do, however, offer brake maintenance at our Gatineau workshop only, with no roadside service.
items.intro: We carry out visual inspections in the workshop or on the road, with a moisture meter, to better target areas vulnerable to water. It is a useful service when you want to confirm the watertight condition of an RV, travel trailer, or caravan.
items.detailIntro: An effective inspection does not mean checking everything without priorities. At Clinique du VR, we focus first on what most often causes costly damage: worn seals, roof defects, vulnerable openings, and signs of water intrusion. The moisture meter helps us confirm problem areas and better guide the next steps.
highlights: Focus on seals and roof condition
Search for signs of water intrusion
included: Visual inspection of seals, sealants and openings
Moisture meter readings in risk areas
Visible condition of the roof and sensitive points
Search for signs of water intrusion or moisture
Observation of weak areas around openings and joints
situations: When you suspect water intrusion
When you want to verify seal or roof condition
Before planning sealing or water-damage repairs
When you want to confirm a suspicious risk area quickly
items.processIntro: Our process is simple and focused on watertightness. The goal is to confirm the visible condition of the seals and roof, detect signs of moisture, and clearly tell you whether a repair should be considered quickly.
process: Initial discussion and inspection type confirmation
Visual inspection of seals, roof and sensitive areas
Moisture meter testing in higher-risk zones
Clear feedback on water intrusion signs and recommended next steps
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for water-intrusion inspection?
items.whyChooseIntro: Water intrusion is one of the most expensive problems on an RV. A useful inspection therefore has to get straight to the point: confirm the condition of the seals, inspect the roof, and measure moisture in the areas that age poorly.

whyChoose.title: Focus on water
whyChoose.text: We focus the inspection on the areas most often affected by water intrusion instead of diluting the service into a general check.

whyChoose.title: Moisture meter included
whyChoose.text: Using the moisture meter helps better confirm the presence of moisture in risk areas and supports the visual findings.

whyChoose.title: Clear pricing
whyChoose.text: $49.99 in the workshop and $149.99 on the road, including a 40 km radius from the office. Contact us for travel farther away.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV inspection

faqs.question: How much does an RV inspection cost?
faqs.answer: The price is $49.99 for an inspection in the workshop and $149.99 for an inspection on the road. The mobile rate includes a 40 km radius from the office. Contact us for travel farther away.

faqs.question: What do you mainly check during an inspection?
faqs.answer: We mainly focus on seals, roof condition and signs of water intrusion. We also use a moisture meter in higher-risk areas.

faqs.question: Do you perform mechanical inspections?
faqs.answer: No. We do not perform mechanical inspections. If your concern is mainly watertightness, see our RV inspection page. We do, however, offer brake maintenance at our shop only, with no mobile brake service.

items.title: RV Maintenance and Seal Service
items.h1: RV Maintenance and Seal Service in Gatineau
items.serviceType: RV Maintenance, Seals and Roof Care
items.seoTitle: RV Maintenance in Gatineau | Seals, Roof and Water Leak Prevention
items.seoDescription: RV maintenance in Gatineau for wall and roof seals, membrane care and water leak prevention. Available in shop or mobile for Gatineau, Outaouais, Ottawa and the Laurentides.
items.opening: RV maintenance in Gatineau at Clinique du VR focuses on replacing wall seals, adding sealant where it is missing on the roof, and maintaining TPO or EPDM roof membranes to prevent water intrusion. We work on sealing areas and roof surfaces that age poorly in order to protect the vehicle against moisture-related damage.
items.lead: Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining the TPO or EPDM roof membrane are excellent ways to prevent water intrusion. We offer this service in the workshop or on the road.
items.intro: We take care of seal maintenance, sensitive roof areas, and TPO or EPDM roof membranes to help preserve the watertightness of an RV, travel trailer, or caravan. The goal is to correct weak areas before they cause more significant damage.
items.detailIntro: Effective maintenance should focus first on the components that protect the vehicle from water. Wall seals, roof seals, TPO or EPDM roof membranes, and exposed sealing points are often the first areas to wear out. Replacing or maintaining them at the right time helps reduce the risk of water intrusion and the larger repairs that can follow.
included: Replacement of worn wall seals
Addition of sealant on the roof and exposed areas where it is missing
Maintenance of TPO or EPDM roof membranes
Visual check of water-sensitive points
Preventive maintenance to reduce intrusion risk
Service available in shop or mobile
situations: When seals start drying, cracking or lifting
When you want to prevent water intrusion before the season
When roof or wall sealing shows visible wear
When you want the work done in shop or directly on site
items.processIntro: Our approach is simple: target the seals and roof areas that truly protect the vehicle from water. We assess the condition of the sealing, replace what needs to be replaced, and point out the next areas to watch to keep the RV watertight.
process: Assessment of wall seals, roof seals, and exposed areas
Seal maintenance or replacement based on condition
Work completed in shop or mobile depending on your situation
Clear feedback on treated areas and what should be monitored next
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for seal maintenance?
items.whyChooseIntro: When you want to avoid water intrusion, you have to act in the right place and at the right time. Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining the membrane are essential interventions for preserving the vehicle's watertightness and avoiding much more costly work later.

whyChoose.title: Water-focused service
whyChoose.text: We focus on seals and roof areas because they are key to preventing water intrusion.

whyChoose.title: Flexible service
whyChoose.text: The service can be carried out at the workshop or on the road depending on the type of need and the location of the vehicle.

whyChoose.title: Cost-saving prevention
whyChoose.text: Replacing seals at the right time often costs much less than a repair after water intrusion.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV maintenance

faqs.question: Why replace RV seals?
faqs.answer: Replacing wall seals, adding roof sealant where it is missing, and maintaining the membrane help preserve the watertight condition of the vehicle and reduce the risk of water intrusion before it causes more serious damage. See our RV maintenance page for the full service details.

faqs.question: What maintenance items matter most here?
faqs.answer: Wall seals, roof areas where the sealant is missing or tired, the roof membrane, and other water-exposed points are among the most important items to maintain regularly. Our resource on twice-yearly RV maintenance also explains the best rhythm to follow.

faqs.question: Do you offer the service in shop or mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer seal maintenance in shop or as a mobile service, depending on what works best for your situation.

items.title: Specialized RV Repair
items.seoTitle: RV Water Leak Repair in Gatineau | Roof, Walls and Flooring
items.seoDescription: RV water leak repair in Gatineau for roof damage, membrane issues, walls, flooring, moisture and mold. Insurance and private repairs for Gatineau, Ottawa, Outaouais and the Laurentides.
items.eyebrow: Water intrusion, roof and interior damage
items.opening: Clinique du VR handles specialized RV repair in Gatineau for problems related to water intrusion, mold, the roof, the roof membrane, flooring, and wall covering. We work on both insurance and private repairs, with special attention to the true source of the damage.
items.lead: When water gets into an RV, the damage often goes beyond the visible surface. The repair approach needs to address the cause, the affected materials and the long-term condition of the vehicle.
items.intro: We carry out specialized repairs on the areas most exposed to water damage, including the roof, roof membrane, floor, walls and interior finish materials.
items.detailIntro: Water intrusion can affect structure, interior materials and the overall condition of the RV. A stain, mold odour, soft floor or damaged wall covering can point to a more advanced issue that requires methodical repair work.
included: Search for the source of water intrusion
Treatment of moisture-affected or mold-affected areas
Roof and roof membrane repair
Repair or replacement of damaged flooring
Wall covering repair and interior restoration
Insurance or private repairs depending on your file
situations: Water stain, mold odour or soft wall
Damaged roof or roof membrane
Damaged floor, ceiling or wall covering
Damage requiring an insurance or private repair
items.processIntro: Our specialized repair method starts by confirming the source of the damage, then follows a logical restoration order. This makes it possible to correct the problem at its source and adapt the work whether it is an insurance file or a private repair.
process: Diagnosis to confirm the source of the leak or damage
Assessment of affected areas such as the roof, floor or walls
Repair and restoration based on the scope of the file
Clear follow-up for insurance or private repair
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for specialized repair?
items.whyChooseIntro: The difference between a durable repair and a simple visual correction comes down to diagnosis, execution quality, and the ability to treat water damage as a whole.

whyChoose.title: Repair focused on the cause
whyChoose.text: We look for the true origin of the water intrusion before closing or rebuilding surfaces.

whyChoose.title: Water-damage expertise
whyChoose.text: We focus on the areas most often affected by moisture, including the roof, membrane, floor and wall systems.

whyChoose.title: Insurance or private work
whyChoose.text: We adapt the repair approach to your situation, whether the file is insurance-based or handled privately.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV repair

faqs.question: What should I do if my RV has a water leak?
faqs.answer: It should be checked quickly so the source can be confirmed. Start with an inspection when the cause is still unclear, or go directly to our RV repair page if damage has already reached the structure, floor, walls, roof membrane, or interior materials while also increasing the risk of mold.

faqs.question: Do you repair roofs and roof membranes?
faqs.answer: Yes. We perform targeted roof and roof membrane repairs when appropriate, depending on material condition and how extensive the damage is. If you want to better recognize the signs first, see our RV water intrusion guide.

faqs.question: Do you handle insurance and private repairs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We handle both insurance-based and private repairs depending on the nature of the damage and the needs of the file. You can see the scope on our specialized RV repair page.

items.intro: RV detailing has to account for larger formats, exposed surfaces, road film, and long storage periods. We focus on cleaning, polishing, and waxing to improve the vehicle's overall appearance.
items.detailIntro: This service is designed for RV and motorhome owners who want deeper cleaning, stronger visual improvement, better gloss, and a more polished finish. Depending on the vehicle, context, and site access, the work can happen in the workshop or as an on-location assignment.

items.title: RV buying and selling
items.h1: RV buying and selling in Gatineau
items.seoTitle: RV buying and selling in Gatineau | Inspection before a transaction
items.seoDescription: Help with selling and buying an RV in Gatineau: pre-transaction inspection, pre-sale preparation, and prioritization of corrections for Gatineau, Ottawa, and Outaouais.
items.opening: The RV buying and selling service in Gatineau helps clarify the vehicle's condition before a transaction, prepare the RV for listing, and guide the corrections that have the most impact. Clinique du VR works to reduce gray areas, better frame the purchase, and improve the credibility of a vehicle put up for sale.
items.lead: For both buying and selling, confidence depends on a vehicle that has been well evaluated and well prepared. We help you see more clearly before making an important decision.
items.intro: We help clarify the RV's condition before a transaction and better prepare the vehicle for the buyer or for your own buying decision.
items.detailIntro: This service is useful when the buyer wants to reduce risk, when the seller wants to know which corrections are the most worthwhile, or when the owner is hesitating between repairing, selling, or keeping the vehicle. The idea is to make the transaction simpler and better informed.
included: Pre-purchase inspection with strengths and weaknesses
Identification of useful work before listing
General vehicle preparation for better presentation
Guidance on priority corrections
Support to make a clearer decision
situations: Buyer who wants to limit surprises
Seller who wants to present a clean and credible RV
Client hesitating between repairing, selling, or buying
Transaction where the vehicle's actual condition must be clarified
items.processIntro: We adapt the support depending on whether you are the buyer or seller. In both cases, the logic stays the same: understand the vehicle's condition, identify the priorities, and clarify the next useful step before making a decision.
process: Discussion about the selling or buying context
Vehicle inspection or preparation
Summary of the points to correct or highlight
Recommended next step based on your objective
items.whyChooseTitle: Why choose Clinique du VR for RV buying and selling?
items.whyChooseIntro: A transaction goes better when the vehicle's condition is understood and documented. That is what helps set a more coherent price, negotiate with more confidence, or avoid a bad purchase.

whyChoose.title: Objective reading
whyChoose.text: We help distinguish cosmetic details from the real technical or structural issues.

whyChoose.title: Targeted preparation
whyChoose.text: Before the sale, we focus efforts on the points that build the buyer's confidence the most.

whyChoose.title: Clearer decision
whyChoose.text: Before purchase, you get a better view of the vehicle's strengths, limits, and potential work.

items.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about RV buying and selling

faqs.question: Why inspect an RV before buying it?
faqs.answer: A pre-purchase inspection helps detect watertightness, roof, system, or presentation issues that can influence the vehicle's actual value.

faqs.question: Which repairs are worth doing before selling an RV?
faqs.answer: The most useful work is the work that clarifies the vehicle's condition, corrects major issues, and improves the general presentation without unnecessary overinvestment. Depending on the case, an inspection or RV detailing may be worth doing before selling.

faqs.question: Can you help if I am not sure whether to repair or sell?
faqs.answer: Yes. This service is specifically meant to prioritize the corrections and better understand whether the vehicle deserves a full restoration, a quick sale, or a more gradual upgrade. See our RV buying and selling page for how we guide that decision.
